course_title: American Political Film and Literature: Conspiracies & Controversies
description:
Offered: every fourth semester
This course introduces students to divisive American political controversies of the past century and equips them to analyze Hollywood’s representations of these pivotal events, as well as political discourse in literature. In particular, you will examine cinematic and literary portrayals of the political process, labor and class struggles, and real and imagined government conspiracies. This course is writing intensive; students will write several short film analysis papers, as well as two longer, researched analysis papers.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, LI
